book1_quote: Sometimes the longest journey begins with an inner call.
book1_p1: After a loss, the heroine decides to leave her old life and set out on a journey that begins as an escape and gradually turns into a calling. Jungles, ancient signs, chance encounters and dreams form an invisible map leading to Paititi — the legendary lost city.
book1_p2: But the deeper the path leads her into the heart of the Amazon, the clearer it becomes: this is a path not only through the forests, but also into herself. Through fear, memory, pain and the awakening of the soul.
book1_p3: Wanderers of the Spirit. Call of Paititi is an atmospheric mystical adventure novel about spiritual search, signs of fate and inner transformation. It is the first book of a trilogy about a path that begins with one step into the unknown.
book2_quote: A journey in which each step opens a living world of Tawaintisuyo
book2_p1: In the second book Wanderers of the Spirit. Guardians of Tawaintisuyo, Karina's path begins where doubt ends. Having heard the call of Paititi and touched the spirit of Yage, she can no longer return to her former life.
book2_p2: The road leads her through Ecuador and Peru, through the cold passes of the Andes — to where the earth holds the memory of ancient civilizations. It is a path of trials, encounters and inner shifts, in which every step becomes part of a greater design.
book2_p3: Gradually the world of Tawaintisuyo opens before Karina — not as a story from textbooks, but as a living reality. Here the mountains speak, signs come in dreams, and chance encounters turn out to be knots of a greater path.
book2_p4: This book is about a journey through space and time, about the awakening of the memory of the earth and how the call of Paititi becomes the call of one's own soul.
book3_quote: The path through the silence of the jungle, where each sign has a meaning
book3_p1: In the third book the path of Karina leads her to a place where roads end and common meanings lose their footing. She enters the primitive forests of Manu — a world where the forests live by their own laws and man returns to being only part of the great breath of the earth.
book3_p2: Among the Machigenga people, on the border with Brazil, Karina confronts reality in which everything has a sign and presence. Here encounters with animals are no longer casual. The mysterious and powerful maruma appears. And the jaguar begins to walk beside her — silently, as a shadow, as a test, whose meaning opens up not immediately.
book3_p3: Step by step Karina approaches the mystery of Paititi. But the closer she gets, the clearer it becomes: the heart of Paititi does not hurry to open itself to those who seek it only on a map.
